answer: To open the config GUI, use `/na`
Click and hold the HUD element and use your scroll wheel to scale it.

The delay is ping based so the median value should be around 150 - ping. If this result is negative then you probably can use 0 delay!
Random delay is based on a Gaussian Distribution so your min and max should usually be around 80% - 120% of the calculated median respectively.

For example: if you have 100 ping it will be 150 - 100 = 50. So your delay range should be 40 - 60ms.
You can get custom names and player sizes by donating to the mod. Ping <@601764042704683040> regarding how to donate.

If you want help, first you need to provide sufficient information so others can understand the problem. Upload relevant crash reports, `latest.log`, or `hs_err_pid<n>.log` files straight into Discord or to https://mclo.gs/ and send the result in a place like #general with context about your issue — someone will probably help you.

Features in the cheat version of NA are made to be spec-safe and watchdog-safe, so probably not. But be sure to think about the settings you use. Also understand that every mod has some inherent risk involved, no matter if it's a cheat or not.

GitHub Actions are like betas of the mod you can download between releases. To get one:
1. Go to the NA GitHub
2. Log into GitHub
3. Go to the **Actions** tab at the top
4. Click the latest action applicable to your version (e.g. `main` branch or `1.21.11` branch)
5. Download the zip file at the bottom and extract it
6. Use the respective `.jar` mod in the folder relevant to you (cheat or legit)
